summary: Matched HSD_ByteCodeEval in sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c with small signature/type cleanup in bytecode.h and the robj.c extern, plus simplification of generated-looking expression/control-flow shapes inside the bytecode evaluator.

key_files.role: Primary matched function implementation; edits HSD_ByteCodeEval locals/signature, fmodf helper, and several expression/control-flow shapes.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c added 25 deleted 39 hunks 4
raw_diff: bytecode.c hunks for fmodf and HSD_ByteCodeEval
human_pr_text: Matches HSD_ByteCodeEval in sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c

key_files.role: Owning header prototype updated to match recovered HSD_ByteCodeEval signature types.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: bytecode.h changes float* args/int nb_args to f32* args/s32 nb_args
changed_files.jsonl: src/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.h added 1 deleted 1

key_files.role: Caller-side extern declaration updated to the same HSD_ByteCodeEval signature types.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: robj.c extern float HSD_ByteCodeEval(u8*, f32*, s32)
changed_files.jsonl: src/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.c added 1 deleted 1

what_changed.text: HSD_ByteCodeEval was reported as newly matching, moving main/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode HSD_ByteCodeEval from 99.92% to 100.00% in the bot report.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report, 1 new match, HSD_ByteCodeEval 99.92% to 100.00%

what_changed.text: The HSD_ByteCodeEval prototype and related extern were changed from float*/int or u32-style parameters to f32* args and s32 nb_args.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: bytecode.c signature changed to float HSD_ByteCodeEval(u8* bytecode, f32* args, s32 nb_args)
raw_diff: bytecode.h prototype changed to f32* args, s32 nb_args
raw_diff: robj.c extern changed to f32*, s32

what_changed.text: The local fmodf helper was rewritten from a compact ternary expression into explicit divisor/dividend parameters, a quotient local, and an early return branch.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fmodf hunk in bytecode.c

what_changed.text: Generated-looking nested blocks around atan2f/RAD_TO_DEG were flattened without changing the visible arithmetic branches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: case 0x30/0x33 region flattening nested braces around fabsf_bitwise, 90.0F, -90.0F, atan2f

what_changed.text: A verbose boolean/XOR-like branch ladder was simplified into a direct boolean expression: (d0 == 0 && d1 != 0) || (d0 != 0 && d1 == 0).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: case 0x32 hunk replaces bool c/val ladder

smart_moves.text: Kept the signature cleanup synchronized across implementation, public header, and the robj.c extern instead of only changing the function body.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: bytecode.c, bytecode.h, robj.c all update HSD_ByteCodeEval parameter types

smart_moves.text: Reduced generated-looking nested scopes and temporary boolean variables in favor of clearer source-level expressions while still landing a reported 100% function match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: nested atan2f block removal
raw_diff: case 0x32 boolean expression simplification
text_corpus: bot report shows HSD_ByteCodeEval after 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matched function body proves a cleaner signature type, update the owning header and visible extern declarations together so call surfaces stay consistent.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_ByteCodeEval signature in bytecode.c
raw_diff: matching prototype update in bytecode.h
raw_diff: matching extern update in robj.c

decomp_lessons.lesson: Generated-looking nested blocks and boolean temporaries can sometimes be replaced by ordinary structured C expressions in a matching PR, as shown by the flattened atan2f branch and direct XOR-like boolean assignment.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: nested brace cleanup around fabsf_bitwise/atan2f
raw_diff: bool c/val ladder replaced with direct boolean expression
text_corpus: bot report shows HSD_ByteCodeEval reached 100.00%

naming_conventions.text: The PR used platform typedef spellings f32 and s32 for the HSD_ByteCodeEval signature and related locals instead of raw float/int in several places.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: float* args/int nb_args changed to f32* args/s32 nb_args
raw_diff: operand_count and d0/d1 changed to s32

naming_conventions.text: Parameter names in the local fmodf helper became divisor and dividend, but the review comment notes Codex-driven renaming may have been accidental and could be cleaned up.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fmodf(f32 divisor, f32 dividend)
review_comments: idk why codex insisted on renaming this everywhere. can clean up if preferred

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use an explicit quotient local and early return in a float modulo helper when a compact ternary form does not match the desired codegen.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fmodf changed from return ternary to quotient local and branch

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Inline the second stack float load directly into the helper call to reduce an extra temporary assignment where codegen requires it.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: f1 assignment removed before fv = fmodf(f0, ((ByteCodeVal*) &stack->data)->f)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Flatten unnecessary nested scopes and express select-like logic directly when the resulting source still matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: nested scope removal around atan2f branch
raw_diff: direct boolean expression for case 0x32
text_corpus: bot report HSD_ByteCodeEval 100.00%

review_feedback.text: The only loaded review comment is from the PR author, noting that Codex appeared to have renamed something broadly and offering cleanup if preferred.
review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
evidence_refs: review_comments: idk why codex insisted on renaming this everywhere. can clean up if preferred
review_feedback.resolution: No explicit resolution is present in the loaded slice.

bytecode evaluator generated nested block cleanup atan2f RAD_TO_DEG
case 0x32 boolean expression d0 d1 HSD_ByteCodeEval

notes: The slice contains the complete raw diff and extracted text records needed for intake.
Verification commands are author-reported; the bot report independently states one new match for HSD_ByteCodeEval, but no raw objdiff output is loaded.
The loaded review feedback is an author comment, not clear external reviewer direction.
The renaming comment is vague; it should not be generalized into a naming standard without more context.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Synchronize recovered function signatures across implementation, header, and extern users
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2484 changed HSD_ByteCodeEval to f32* args/s32 nb_args in bytecode.c, bytecode.h, and the robj.c extern while matching the function. This supports a reusable lesson to keep proven signatures consistent across declarations when decomping a function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: bytecode.c signature update
raw_diff: bytecode.h prototype update
raw_diff: robj.c extern update
text_corpus: bot report HSD_ByteCodeEval 100.00%

accepted_candidate_records.title: Replace generated-looking boolean ladders with direct boolean expressions when verified
accepted_candidate_records.text: In HSD_ByteCodeEval case 0x32, a verbose bool c/val ladder was replaced by the direct expression (d0 == 0 && d1 != 0) || (d0 != 0 && d1 == 0), and the bot report showed the function reaching 100%.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: case 0x32 boolean ladder replacement
text_corpus: bot report HSD_ByteCodeEval 100.00%

source_update_candidates.title: Header signature updates should include related extern declarations
source_update_candidates.text: When a function body match proves more precise typedefs or parameter types, update the owning header and any visible extern declarations in touched callers together. PR 2484 demonstrates this with HSD_ByteCodeEval changed consistently in bytecode.c, bytecode.h, and robj.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: bytecode.c, bytecode.h, robj.c HSD_ByteCodeEval signature updates
source_update_candidates.reason: This is consistent with the existing truthful-headers-and-includes standard, but could be added as a concrete extern-declaration example if the curator wants more specificity.

rejection_notes: Do not promote divisor/dividend naming from fmodf as an accepted naming convention; the author comment explicitly casts some Codex-driven renaming as potentially accidental.
Do not infer that all bytecode evaluator operations should use f32/s32 typedefs globally; the PR evidence only shows HSD_ByteCodeEval and related declarations.
Do not claim external reviewer approval or requested cleanup beyond the loaded author review comment.
